misconceptionSummary: Students use the wrong input in the nth-term rule or evaluate the algebraic expression incorrectly.

steps.narration: This is wrong because the input was not the position number.
The fourth term means n should be four.

lines.text: The mistake came from using the wrong input value for n

steps.narration: The rule itself was fine. The input was wrong.

visuals.expression: Use the term number as n every time
visuals.label: Correct input gives the correct term

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, write n equals the term position before substituting.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: substituting a term value instead of the term number. In nth-term rules, n is always the position.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Anchor yourself by writing the term number first, then substitute that into the rule.
